titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/156

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=431 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:17:06.904588+00 | translation moa=432 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:17:08.238768+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Gaufridus filius Walleui de Lilles
cliue Salutem . Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse et hac mea Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie
Sancte Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam puram et perpetuam
Elemosinam tres bouatas terre arabilis In territorio de Wittun sicut iacent in uno
tenemento desuper Rauenesfen propinquiores terre eorundem monachorum quam habent ex do
natione Patricii de Ridale domini meo . Totam hanc terram et quicquid continetur infra diui
sas predicte terre dedi predictis monachis de Melros libera . quieta et soluta ab omni terreno seruitio
et consuetudine et exactione seculari ut ea habeant . teneant et possideant in perpetuum ita libere
plene et honorifice sicut aliqua Elemosina liberius . quietius plenius et honorificentius teneri potest et
possideri . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus prefatis monachis ean
dem terram contra omnes homines qui uiuunt et mori poterunt . et adquietabimus de omni forensi ser
uitio erga dominos nostros et de omnibus aliis rebus que pro aliqua terra exigi poterunt in perpetuum .
Hiis Test’. Roberto . Glasguensi . Archidiacono . Patricio de Ridale domino meo . Waltero et Nichol’
filiis eius . Ric’. persona de Alnecrum . Roberto . de Bernoldeb’
Adam de Wittun . Willelmo . de Wittun . Adam de Chattun . et multis aliis .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and to come, Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and perpetual alms, three oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’ near the land of the same monks which they have from the donation of Patrick of Ryedale my lord. This whole land and whatever is comprised within the boundaries of the said land I have given to the said monks of Melrose free, quit and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction, so that they may have it, hold it and possess it for ever, as freely, fully and honourably as any alms can be quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed, and we shall discharge for ever all forinsec service towards our lords and all other things that can be exacted for any land.
